title: Fattoush

overview.description: A Levantine salad of fresh vegetables, herbs and crisp toasted flatbread dressed with lemon, olive oil and sumac.

ingredients: 1 large handful fresh parsley, roughly chopped
1 small handful fresh mint leaves, roughly chopped
method: Tear the pitta breads into rough pieces.
Spread them on a baking tray.
Toast for 8–12 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.
Place the tomatoes, cucumber, radishes, red onion and green pepper in a large bowl.
Add the parsley, mint and spring onions.
Whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, sumac, garlic, salt and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables.
Add the toasted pitta pieces just before serving.
Finish with an extra sprinkling of sumac.

learning.did-you-know: Fattoush is a Levantine bread salad whose name is related to the idea of crumbling or breaking bread into pieces. It is a particularly good way to use leftover flatbread.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to combine fresh vegetables with toasted bread and how sumac and pomegranate molasses create a distinctive sour, fruity dressing.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fresh and inexpensive complete meal salad that uses leftover flatbreads while offering a completely different texture and flavour profile from Greek salad.
chapman-tips: Add the toasted bread immediately before serving so it remains crisp.
Use plenty of herbs; they are an important part of the salad rather than just a garnish.
Sumac provides the characteristic sharp, lemony flavour.
Pomegranate molasses adds depth and a subtle fruity sweetness.
The vegetables can be prepared ahead, but keep the dressing and toasted bread separate until serving.
Pitta is convenient, but leftover flatbread works very well.
For a more substantial meal, serve with hummus and falafel or grilled chicken.
A little extra sumac at the table is always welcome.
notes.batch-cooking: The dressed vegetables are better fresh. Toasted bread should be stored separately and added just before serving.
notes.leftovers: Keep the bread separate if possible. Undressed vegetables can be used in wraps or served alongside hummus.
